SOURCE_LANGUAGE: JavaScript
DOC FORMAT: JSDoc

SYNTAX
======
- Block starts with /** on its own line, every continuation line with
  ' * ', closes with ' */'.
- Indentation matches the function/class/method declaration exactly.
- First line: concise, active one-liner. NO 'This function ...'.
- Blank line before the block tags.

BLOCK TAGS (in this order, only when applicable)
================================================
@param   {Type} name             <description ending with a period.>
@param   {Type} [name=default]   <optional parameter with default.>
@param   {Type} [name]           <optional parameter without default.>
@returns {Type}                  <description ending with a period.>
@throws  {ErrorType}             <reason.>
@async                           (for async functions)
@deprecated                      <reason + migration hint.>
@see     <reference>
@example <short code example>

TYPE EXPRESSION RULES
=====================
- Primitives: {string}, {number}, {boolean}, {null}, {undefined}, {bigint}, {symbol}
- Object/Array: {Object}, {Object<string, number>}, {Array<string>}, {string[]}
- Function types: {function(string, number): boolean}
- Union: {string|number}
- Optional in type position: {string=}    (alternative to bracket form)
- Nullable: {?string}
- Non-nullable: {!string}
- Generics for classes: {Promise<User>}, {Map<string, User>}
- 'Anything': {*}    (use sparingly - only when truly any)

RULES
=====
- For async functions, use @async AND describe the resolved value via
  @returns {Promise<T>}.
- For object/options parameters, document sub-properties:
    @param {Object} options
    @param {string} options.name
    @param {number} [options.timeout=5000]
- Class constructors: @class and @constructor are redundant - omit them.
- For ES module exports: do not place @module on each function, only once
  per file.
- TypeScript-style annotations as @type comments are out of scope - use
  .ts files for that.

EXAMPLE
=======
/**
 * Calculates the gross total including value-added tax.
 *
 * @param {Array<{price: number, quantity: number}>} items Line items to total.
 * @param {number} taxRate Tax rate as a decimal (e.g. `0.19`).
 * @returns {number} Gross total as a Number.
 * @throws {RangeError} When `items` is empty.
 */
